steps.title: Find the growth factor for a 3-unit change
steps.content:
Rewrite $f(x)=a\cdot b^{x/n}$ as $f(x)=a\cdot k^x$, where $k=b^{1/n}$ is the multiplier for each 1-unit increase in $x$. Ratios eliminate $a$:
$$\frac{f(6)}{f(3)}=\frac{216}{8}=27$$
The input increased by $3$, so $k^3=27$ and $k=3$.

steps.title: Use the exponential multiplier
steps.content: The per-unit multiplier is $3$, so each increase of $1$ in $x$ multiplies the function value by $3$. This avoids needing to find $a$, $b$, or $n$ separately.

steps.title: Compute f(8) using the growth factor
steps.content:
From $x=6$ to $x=8$ is a 2-unit increase, so multiply by $3^2=9$:
$$f(8)=216\cdot 9=1944$$
The answer is 1944.
